items.text: Confirm docs/specpad/ holds specpad.proj.json, specpad.srs.json, and specpad.vtp.json, and that each parses as JSON.
items.expected: All three files exist under docs/specpad/ and parse without error.

items.text: Run validate() on a document missing schemaVersion and on one with an unknown type.
items.expected: Each is reported as structurally invalid (non-empty error list).

items.text: Run validate() on a project document that has name and title.
items.expected: validate() returns no errors.

items.text: Run validate() on an SRS item missing text, and on one missing id.
items.expected: Both are reported invalid, each error naming the missing required field.

items.text: Run validate() on a VTP item with result "maybe" and one with result "passed".
items.expected: The "maybe" item is invalid (result enum); the "passed" item is valid.

items.text: Create an item with the factory, then serialize and parse a document; compare item ids before and after.
items.expected: createSrsItem assigns an id, and the load/save round-trip preserves every id unchanged.

items.text: Generate ids with generateId and inspect ID_PREFIX.
items.expected: Each id matches prefix_<6 hex>, and ID_PREFIX maps requirement→r, test→t, heading→h.

items.text: In the VTP editor, set a test's verifies to a requirement and inspect the stored value.
items.expected: The verifies entry equals the requirement's id, not its code label.

items.text: Change a requirement's code label and re-check a test that verifies it.
items.expected: The requirement's id is unchanged and the verifying test still resolves.
items.notes: Manual; id immutability is enforced because code is editable but id is not.

items.text: Render the SRS table for a requirement with one, then two, verifying tests and inspect the saved file.
items.expected: The displayed count is computed from verifies (1, then 2); no numTests field is written to disk.

items.text: Inspect a saved SpecPad document for history fields.
items.expected: No modifiedUser, modifiedDate, or change-list keys are present; the file validates.

items.text: Call validate() with documents of each type.
items.expected: validate() selects the schema by doc.type and returns [] for each valid document.

items.text: Validate a structurally-correct SRS+VTP pair that has an untested requirement.
items.expected: validate() returns no errors (policy is not its concern); the traceability gap is found only by governance.

items.text: Run checkGovernance on an SRS+VTP pair where one requirement has no verifying test.
items.expected: A traceability violation is reported for the untested requirement.

items.text: Run checkGovernance where a test verifies a non-existent requirement id.
items.expected: A referential-integrity violation is reported for that test.

items.text: Run checkGovernance on a non-heading test whose expected value is empty.
items.expected: A missing-expected violation is reported for that test.

items.text: Run checkGovernance on a bundle whose only untested requirement is a heading.
items.expected: No traceability violation is reported for the heading.

items.text: Compare the shared module's GOVERNANCE_RULES ids against the skill's SKILL.md.
items.expected: Every governance rule id appears in SKILL.md (parity); drift fails the test.

items.text: Round-trip an SRS document through serialize/parse and exercise the fallback download path.
items.expected: The document round-trips with a trailing newline; a fallback open/download path exists for unsupported browsers.

items.text: Render the SRS table with a VTP that verifies a requirement and read the Tests column.
items.expected: The verifying-test count is shown and updates as more tests verify the requirement.

items.text: In the Testing view, change a test's result and save.
items.expected: onSave receives a VTP document with the updated result on the correct item.

items.text: Render the validation panel for a clean bundle and for one with an untested requirement.
items.expected: The panel shows "No problems" when clean and a traceability message when a requirement is untested.

items.text: Render the VTP table with a test whose verifies points at an unknown id.
items.expected: The offending row is visually flagged (danger styling).

items.text: Open a project with two documents and switch between them in the picker.
items.expected: The editor re-seeds the table for the newly selected document; it does not write the prior document's content.

items.text: Follow SKILL.md to scaffold docs/specpad/ from the templates and add items.
items.expected: The folder is scaffolded with PROJECT_NAME replaced and newly added items receive unique stable ids.
items.notes: Manual dogfood; this project's docs/specpad/ was produced this way.

items.text: Run validate() and checkGovernance() over SpecPad's own SpecPad documents.
items.expected: validate() returns no errors and checkGovernance() returns no violations for the SpecPad docs.

items.text: Inspect the generated launcher index.html.
items.expected: It redirects to https://specpad.com/v01/?name=...&open=... using the project name.

items.text: Compute buildRedline(baseline, working) with an added and a modified item, then render the SRS table with the resulting redline.
items.expected: Added rows are flagged, modified rows are flagged with the changed cell highlighted, and removed items are listed in a panel.

items.text: Diff two document versions where a code label is renamed and items are reordered.
items.expected: No spurious add/remove; a renamed code is a single modified entry; pure reordering yields no change.

items.text: Inspect SKILL.md for the change-tracking section.
items.expected: It states the skill never computes diffs and that the editor computes all change views (one diff implementation).

items.text: Run the skill refresh against a tagged repo and inspect docs/specpad/.specpad/.
items.expected: The cache holds verbatim snapshot copies plus a release manifest; no computed-diff files are written.

items.text: Run validate() on a releases manifest and exercise cachedReleases().
items.expected: The manifest validates with each release carrying version, ref, date, and an author {name,email}; cachedReleases maps the baseline release to the baseline location.

items.text: Run computeAttribution over ordered snapshots, then format with attributionLabel.
items.expected: Each item resolves addedIn/lastChangedIn with the release author, and the oldest cached snapshot is marked as a boundary.

items.text: Render VersionTimeline with a manifest of two releases.
items.expected: Both versions render with their dates and authors, and the baseline release is flagged.

items.text: Set a job in JobControl and inspect SKILL.md for the trailer convention.
items.expected: JobControl reports the entered job id and title to its handler; SKILL.md documents folding the current job into a Job: commit trailer; no job field is stored on items.

items.text: Open a project whose change-tracking cache is absent (loaders return null).
items.expected: The editor renders and remains editable, showing a "history unavailable — run specpad refresh" note instead of redline/attribution.
items.notes: LocalApp degraded path: loaders return null when the cache is absent (src/__tests__/LocalApp.test.tsx mocks them null); an explicit banner assertion is still pending.

items.text: Refresh a tagged repo, then pull an older version's snapshot on demand.
items.expected: Refresh caches the latest release snapshot; an older snapshot is written to the cache only when explicitly pulled.
items.notes: Manual dogfood: baseline cached by refresh, v0.1 pulled on demand; skill/__tests__/change-tracking.test.ts documents pull.

items.text: Open a requirement's hamburger menu and choose Add ▸ Child.
items.expected: A new requirement is inserted directly below at level + 1.

items.text: Render the SRS table containing a heading that has a code segment.
items.expected: The heading's Code cell shows the derived dotted path; the literal word "heading" never appears.

items.text: Open a row's hamburger menu.
items.expected: It lists add (above/below/child), add heading, indent/outdent, delete, and view information; delete confirms; outdent is disabled at level 0.

items.text: Click the show-tests toggle on a requirement that has a verifying test.
items.expected: The verifying VTP tests are listed inline beneath the requirement.

items.text: Render the SRS table and open a requirement's information modal.
items.expected: No Hazards column is present in the table; the info modal shows the item's hazards.

items.text: Build the redline rows for a baseline vs a working doc with an added, a modified, and a removed item.
items.expected: Added/modified rows are flagged and the removed row renders inline struck-through at its baseline position; no separate removed panel.

items.text: Drag a requirement row onto another position.
items.expected: The items reorder to reflect the drop position.
items.notes: Manual; HTML5 draggable rows in SRSTable; the reorder index math was reviewed but has no DOM drag test.

items.text: Render the editor menu bar with a project open.
items.expected: It shows the SpecPad brand and the primary controls (File, Save, Job, version) in one bar; no separate page header/toolbar.

items.text: Open the File menu and choose New document; render with FS access unavailable.
items.expected: New document fires; with no FS access the menu offers the upload/download fallback open.

items.text: With two projects in the directory, pick the other from the brand dropdown.
items.expected: The chosen project's documents load.

items.text: Render the view tabs with only an SRS document present.
items.expected: Requirements is active; tabs for absent documents are disabled and do not switch when clicked.

items.text: Render the status bar for a clean bundle and for one with a governance violation.
items.expected: Clean shows "No problems found"; a violation shows an error/warning count that expands to the detail messages on click.

items.text: Click the menu-bar version chip and render the version-history dialog.
items.expected: The chip triggers the dialog; the dialog lists releases, or shows an empty state prompting specpad refresh.

items.text: Edit a row in each table and inspect the change callback.
items.expected: Each table calls onChange with the updated document and renders no internal Save button.

items.text: Render the menu bar clean and then dirty.
items.expected: Save is disabled when clean and enabled with an unsaved indicator when dirty; clicking it saves.
items.notes: src/components/__tests__/MenuBar.test.tsx (Ctrl/Cmd-S wired via the LocalApp keydown effect)

items.text: Switch documents with no pending edits.
items.expected: No prompt appears on a clean switch; a dirty switch prompts before discarding (beforeunload guard exercised manually).
items.notes: src/__tests__/LocalApp.test.tsx (asserts no confirm on a clean switch)

items.text: Open the editor with ?demo and confirm the SpecPad demo project loads with no folder picker, the SRS/VTP/Testing views are populated, and version history opens.
items.expected: The demo project loads read-only over HTTP from /demo/; all read views (tables, testing, validation, version history, redlines, job chip) function.
items.notes: src/__tests__/LocalApp.demo.test.tsx; src/__tests__/localFileApi.demo.test.ts; testing view, version history, and redlines exercised manually in the dev server

items.text: In demo mode, confirm no Save button or File menu is offered, Ctrl+S does nothing, and the read-only indicator is shown.
items.expected: All write affordances are absent or inert and a "Demo — read-only" indicator is visible.

items.text: Run the site build and test suite; confirm every schema field carries a description and the reference HTML includes every field and governance rule.
items.expected: site/src/__tests__/generate-reference.test.ts and src/shared/__tests__/schema-descriptions.test.ts both pass; the build fails if any schema property lacks a description.
items.notes: site/src/__tests__/generate-reference.test.ts; src/shared/__tests__/schema-descriptions.test.ts — automated coverage confirmed in the marketing-site branch test run

items.text: After a deploy, fetch https://specpad.com/specpad-skill.zip and confirm it is a valid zip containing the skill directory rebuilt from the repository.
items.expected: The URL returns a non-empty zip archive whose contents match the skill/ directory at that commit; infra/deploy.sh step rebuilds dist-site/specpad-skill.zip from the repo on every ship.
items.notes: Manual verification 2026-06-10: fetched https://specpad.com/specpad-skill.zip after shipping (application/zip); unzipped cleanly to specpad/SKILL.md + 5 template files matching skill/specpad/. The zip is rebuilt fresh by infra/deploy.sh Ship 4/5 on every deploy.

items.text: Run validate() on a jobs register and on records missing required fields.
items.expected: A well-formed jobs doc validates; a record missing status or title, or with a status outside open/closed, is reported invalid.

items.text: Inspect the jobs register schema and SKILL.md for any stored change associations.
items.expected: The jobsSchema record has only id/code/title/description/status — no list of changed items or commits; SKILL.md documents deriving job → SRS → VTP → source from git (git log --grep + diffDocs).

items.text: Normalize markers via activeJobIds (jobs array and legacy job) and inspect SKILL.md trailer guidance.
items.expected: activeJobIds returns the jobs array when present, falls back to a single job, else []; SKILL.md states one Job: trailer is written per active id, carrying the record id.

items.text: Run checkGovernance with an active marker pointing at a closed record and at an id absent from the register.
items.expected: active-job-open is reported for the closed entry and active-job-known for the dangling entry; open, known entries produce no violation; with no register, neither rule fires.

items.text: Render the Jobs view and the active-job picker; add a job, edit it, toggle the active set, and inspect closed records.
items.expected: The view lists/creates/edits records and multi-selects active jobs; a closed job is never offered for activation; a closed-but-active job can be removed.

items.text: Select a closed job in the Jobs view and attempt to activate it.
items.expected: No activation control is offered for a closed, non-active job; the guidance directs creating a new job; the skill follows the same rule.

items.text: Push a commit that has no Job: trailer through the pre-push hook.
items.expected: The hook exits non-zero and blocks the push, naming the offending commit; a commit carrying a Job: trailer passes.

items.text: Push a commit that changes code but touches no SRS/VTP, with and without a Spec: none trailer.
items.expected: The hook warns (without blocking) and recommends a requirement audit; a Spec: none trailer suppresses the warning. When committing via Claude, the agent proposes a requirement derived from the diff for ratification.
items.notes: skill/__tests__/pre-push.test.ts (warn + Spec: none cases); SKILL.md commit workflow Layer 2 (requirement audit)

items.text: Run the pre-push hook over synthetic commits in a throwaway repo: no-trailer, trailer+spec, code-without-spec, Spec: none, and SPECPAD_SKIP.
items.expected: Block on the no-trailer commit; pass on trailer+spec; warn on code-without-spec; no warning with Spec: none; full bypass with SPECPAD_SKIP=1. Merge commits and pre-adoption history are not policed.

items.text: Inspect SKILL.md for the SpecPad working-loop section.
items.expected: It documents capturing requirements as you build, spec-first, with the job → requirement → test → code chain attributed to the active job.

items.text: Inspect SKILL.md for the distillation guidance.
items.expected: It states 'intent, not transcript', defines what rises to a requirement (testable behavioral 'shall' statements, not implementation detail), and gives the regression-test litmus.

items.text: Inspect SKILL.md for the autonomous-but-visible capture rule, and confirm this feature was itself captured that way.
items.expected: SKILL.md states requirements are written autonomously then surfaced for the user to edit; JOBS-10..12 were drafted spec-first under JOB-1 with verifying tests (self-dogfood).
items.notes: skill/__tests__/working-loop.test.ts; this feature was captured via the loop it defines.

items.text: Inspect SKILL.md for the init procedure and confirm the artifacts it installs exist.
items.expected: SKILL.md documents the four init steps (scaffold, launcher, hook via core.hooksPath, CLAUDE.md wiring); templates/hooks/pre-push and templates/CLAUDE.specpad.md both exist in the skill.

items.text: Check the CLAUDE directive template for an idempotency sentinel and SKILL.md for the safe-rerun rules.
items.expected: templates/CLAUDE.specpad.md carries the <!-- specpad:working-loop --> sentinel; SKILL.md says to append only when the sentinel is absent and not to overwrite existing docs or clobber an existing core.hooksPath.

items.text: Inspect SKILL.md for how init handles an existing CLAUDE.md.
items.expected: SKILL.md states init appends the directive and never overwrites CLAUDE.md, creating it only if absent.
